company_type: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production

dependencies: Independent drilling contractors and third-party service contractors for all drilling and completion activities
Third-party gathering, processing, compression, storage, transportation, refining, liquefaction and export facilities
Availability of water for drilling and completion operations
Availability of drilling rigs, hydraulic fracturing services, pressure pumping equipment, tubular materials, sand, disposal facilities, qualified personnel
risks: Unavailability of third-party gathering, processing, compression, storage, transportation, refining, liquefaction and export facilities
Supply chain disruptions affecting availability of facilities and equipment
Inflationary pressures on costs of fuel, steel, labor, drilling and completion services
Limitations on ability to secure sufficient water due to drought or local restrictions
Shortages or delays in availability of drilling rigs, hydraulic fracturing services, equipment, materials, qualified personnel

regulatory_risks: Federal, state, tribal and local environmental, health and safety regulations
Climate change-related regulations and policies including GHG emissions regulations
U.S. EPA regulations on methane and VOC emissions from oil and gas operations
U.S. EPA final methane rules published March 2024
BLM Waste Prevention Rule requiring leak detection and waste minimization plans
Inflation Reduction Act methane emissions charge on certain oil and gas facilities
State and local regulations on hydraulic fracturing including disclosure requirements and restrictions on chemical additives
Regulations on access to and use of water for drilling operations
Regulations on disposal of produced water, drilling fluids and other wastes
Permitting requirements for oil and gas drilling on state, tribal and federal lands
Leasing restrictions or bans on federal lands for oil and gas development
Royalty calculation regulations for federal and state leases including increased royalty rates
U.S. federal income tax law changes affecting oil and gas companies
Potential elimination of intangible drilling cost deductions
Global minimum tax (GMT) implementation in various jurisdictions
Carbon tax or emissions-related tax proposals
SEC climate-related disclosure rules
California climate-related disclosure mandates
European Union methane emissions limits on crude oil and natural gas imports beginning 2030
Regulations on financial derivative transactions and hedging activities
Import and export regulations for crude oil, natural gas and related commodities
FERC regulations on natural gas sales and transportation
Trinidad government regulations on oil and gas operations and production sharing contracts

sanction_risks: Imposition of tariffs or trade or other economic sanctions
U.S. laws and policies affecting foreign trade and taxation including tariffs and economic sanctions
Potential impact of tariffs on operating costs and capital expenditures
